sections.draft_text:
Fujitsu recognises the critical importance of delivering services in alignment with the Deed and Work Order as specified by the Department of Defence. Our extensive experience in supporting the Australian Defence Organisation positions us uniquely to meet these requirements effectively. We have a proven track record of delivering ICT services across various security domains, including Protected, Secret, and Top Secret environments, which ensures our capability to integrate seamlessly with the Commonwealth's existing ICT infrastructure.

Our approach is underpinned by a robust embedded security model that incorporates role-based access control, data encryption, and user authentication. These security measures are integral to our solution design, system integration, and operational delivery, ensuring compliance with all relevant government and industry standards. This approach guarantees that Defence data remains protected throughout the service lifecycle, aligning with the Commonwealth's security objectives.

Fujitsu's methodology is designed to ensure that all services provided meet the specifications, laws, authorisations, performance measures, and milestones outlined in the Deed and Work Order. We are committed to delivering outcomes that are consistent with ITIL v3.0 standards, ensuring high-quality and consistent service delivery. Our national workforce, which includes over 1,100 security-cleared personnel, enables us to support distributed and complex Defence environments effectively.

In addressing the specific requirements of the RFQ and SOW, Fujitsu will utilise the Maximum Labour Rates as outlined in Annex A to Attachment B to the COD for calculating On Call Allowances. Our performance will be regularly reviewed against the specified Performance Measures, ensuring continuous alignment with the Commonwealth's objectives and facilitating any necessary adjustments to the Work Order.

Fujitsu's long-standing partnership with Defence, coupled with our comprehensive delivery footprint and security-focused approach, ensures that we are well-equipped to minimise risks and disruptions while achieving the objectives set forth in the Deed and Work Order.
